column2: You must really have a penchant for talking to strangers, huh?
We don't mean to intervene with your rest. Please continue while we take our leave.
Wait. Wait. Hold on.
I don't get that many visitors so I might as well entertain myself.
We have no time for this.
What do you have to say to us?
Nothing, really.
I may just have something to give you.
Another potion? Let's go!
We appreciate your willingness to help--
Not so fast!
You'll have to do something for me in return.
What do you expect from us?
Further along the path where you come from, there prowls a pair of Catapaces.
Every time I pass by to go meet Doloris, they howl and bark and lunge as if they can harm a ghost.
I don't like where this is going.
We're listening.
So, I need you to take care of them for me.
In return, I may have something extraordinary for you to reward your efforts.
That's the shadiest offer I've ever heard. We're not getting embroiled in a vendetta between a ghost and some monsters.
Besides, we can't risk our lives for a potion. We're better off moving along.
Your grace. What is your opinion on the matter?
I trust the vanguard's judgement.
We could use any help we get in here.
We may be back.
I hope you are. Don't disappoint me.
I can't give you anything before you finish your job.
I could hear the distinct shreiking of those foul beasts. Well done my little doves.
Here you go. I give you what many a fool have ventured fruitlessly in this contraption for.
You will realize its value when the time comes.
That was the only one in my possesion. I have no more.
Wait, aren't those the same pair of Catapaces we slew on our way here?
A pair of Catapaces had lunged at us on our way here too. We struck them down.
They're gone? Those foul beasts?
Oh my word. I can't contain my joy. I won't have to brace myself for their horrible growls every time I pass by.
Here, here you go. Take this. I'm very happy with you lot.
You will realize its value when the time comes.
